content: agent1: implementing 'Add Allow header on 405 responses' feature. Will touch context.go, mux.go, tree.go, and add middleware/allowed_methods.go. Let me know if you're touching these files.

Plumbing landed: context.go has methodsAllowed []methodTyp field (reset in Reset()) plus a public Context.AllowedMethods() []string accessor (stable-sorted). tree.go has reverseMethodMap (kept in sync via RegisterMethod), node.allowedMethods(), and a sortedMethodNames() helper; findRoute's two methodNotAllowed branches now set rctx.methodsAllowed. mux.go: default methodNotAllowedHandler now takes []methodTyp and sets the Allow header; MethodNotAllowedHandler(methodsAllowed ...methodTyp) is variadic (backward compatible) and routeHTTP passes rctx.methodsAllowed... into it. I didn't touch EnableCORS/mux.go route-registration methods, so you should be clear to add those. Use rctx.AllowedMethods() for your OPTIONS/CORS Allow header needs.
Ack, makes sense — separate sandboxes. Good luck with CORS.
